data.narrative:
Vast is a late-stage Space & Aerospace company founded by Jake Cannell and Christian Horne that has raised $300M+ and carries a current post-money valuation of $2.0B. The peer set comprises 17 comparable companies across both public and private markets, including five public tickers (RKLB, PL, LUNR, ASTS, SATL) and eight private peers such as Relativity Space ($4.2B), Astranis ($3.5B), and ICEYE ($2.8B).

Two valuation methods are applicable. Stage-progression analysis applies a 1.2x step-up multiplier typical for late-stage to next-stage advancement, yielding $2.4B. Peer-median analysis across the 17-company comparable set—which spans a range of $400M to $7.0B—produces a median valuation of $1.3B. Public-comp anchoring via Price-to-Sales multiples cannot be applied, as private company revenue is not disclosed. The sector median P/S for public Space & Aerospace peers is 49.8x across six tickers, but remains inapplicable without Vast's revenue.

Triangulation of the two applicable methods supports a valuation range of $1.1B (low) to $2.8B (high), with a median of $1.9B. Vast's current $2.0B valuation sits near the midpoint, though material uncertainty exists around revenue trajectory, competitive positioning within the crowded commercial space sector, and pathway to profitability—all unresolved by public filings.
